title: Local Responses to Mass Violence: A Comparative Study of Gun Violence Prevention Policies in Colorado
funder: University of Colorado Denver, Office of Research Services (Research Team Grant)
description: Mixed-methods comparative study of how local governments in Colorado adopt and implement gun violence prevention policies in response to mass shootings. Combines policy coding, quantitative outcome analysis, stakeholder interviews, and network analysis across 25 years of Colorado data. Led by Deserai Anderson (PI), Sheila Huss (co-PI), and Alejandra Medina at the CU Denver School of Public Affairs Center for Community Safety and Resilience.
